stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 board meeting and earnings call scheduled for April 29, 2026 (final dividend recommendation expected). Stock around Rs 6,267 on April 22, 2026; market cap ~Rs 32,576 Cr. ESOP allotment of 37,385 equity shares completed on April 21, 2026. Q3 FY26 PAT +121.77% YoY to Rs 185.4 Cr; revenue +47.21% YoY to Rs 892.37 Cr. Jefferies expects Q4 revenue +21% and EBITDA +52%, driven by Project Nectar ramp-up and strong R32 refrigerant demand.
stocks.sector_context: Indian specialty chemicals sector facing H1 FY26 headwinds from US tariffs (R32/R125, MMA, 2,4-D affected), geopolitical issues, weak agrochemical demand. However, fluorochemicals segment remains structurally favored due to limited global competition and strong export demand. Refrigerant pricing environment supportive given HFC phase-down dynamics.
stocks.peer_comparison: Jefferies' top sector pick; JM Financial also picks NAFL among refrigerant gas players. Expected to materially outperform PI Industries (revenue -11%, EBITDA -22% in Q4 estimates). SRF and NAFL preferred over Gujarat Fluorochemicals, Aarti Industries, Atul. NAFL's aggressive growth path differentiates from peers.
red_flags: Highest US revenue exposure in the peer group at ~23% (vs PIIL 15%, UPLL 10-12%, SRF 8%) — tariff sensitivity highest
Jefferies estimates US tariffs could shave ~9% off NAFL FY26 operating profit
Stock at premium valuation; any Q4 miss vs high expectations could drive sharp correction
Pre-earnings event risk — results on April 29 (5 days out)
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results + final dividend on April 29, 2026 — high expectations baked in
Project Nectar continued ramp + Project Hyper capex monetization commentary
R32/HFC pricing tailwind from supply consolidation
Management outlook on FY27 CDMO and CRAMS pipeline

stocks.recent_news: Stock at Rs 757.4 on April 18, 2026; market cap Rs 8,060 Cr; P/E 32.83. Down -28.15% over 6 months and -36.03% over 1 year — sustained underperformance. Leadership reshuffle effective April 1, 2026: Siddhartha Sikchi elevated to MD, Ashok Boob to Executive Vice Chairman, Krishnakumar Boob re-appointed Whole-Time Director. Promoter group internal restructuring: Nilima Boob acquired 6.24% via inter-se gift transfer (holding to 6.33%). Q4 FY26 results upcoming.
stocks.sector_context: Specialty chemicals sector under pressure from US tariffs and weak global demand. MEHQ/Anisole/Guaiacol pricing remains soft amid Chinese competition. Sector outlook for H1 FY26 muted per industry commentary.
stocks.peer_comparison: Significantly lagging peers like Navin Fluorine (which has positive analyst momentum). Stock down 36% over 1 year vs sector that has been mixed-to-positive on select names. Trading at premium P/E (~33x) despite underperformance — valuation still rich for the de-rating.
red_flags: Promoter holding fell from 74.97% (Mar 2025) to 50.96% (Dec 2025) following Aug 2025 on-market sale of 24% stake — major overhang
1-year stock return -36% — among worst in specialty chem peer group
Q2 FY26 results showed profit dip (margins improved but PAT softness)
Leadership transition adds execution uncertainty
Chinese pricing pressure on MEHQ/anisole core products
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results awaited — could trigger sentiment reset either way
New MD Sikchi's strategic direction commentary
Recovery in MEHQ/derivatives pricing if China supply normalizes

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results released April 22-23, 2026 — net profit Rs 842 Cr (+30% YoY); revenue Rs 2,065 Cr (+20% YoY); EBIT margin >50%. Stock surged ~9% post-results to Rs 8,828; market cap Rs 70,729 Cr (April 23). Declared bumper second interim dividend of Rs 270/share (face value Rs 5). Record date May 7, 2026; payment by May 21, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Indian IT/fintech services demand resilient. BFSI tech spending strong as global banks accelerate core modernization and cloud migration. OFSS specifically benefits from Oracle parent's cloud push and FLEXCUBE / banking platform demand.
stocks.peer_comparison: Beat large-cap IT peers on margin (>50% EBIT vs 22-26% for TCS/Infosys/Wipro) and growth (20% revenue vs single-digit for tier-1 IT). Differentiated as a product/IP play vs services majors. Among the strongest BFSI-tech earnings prints this season.
red_flags: Stock already up ~9% on results — much of the positive surprise priced in
Lumpy revenue model can produce quarter-to-quarter volatility
Low free float and limited institutional coverage can amplify moves both ways
catalysts: Dividend ex-date May 7, 2026 — Rs 270/share (yield ~3% on current price)
Continued momentum in licensed product revenue and cloud transition
Oracle global banking platform wins flowing through to OFSS topline
Buyback or special dividend possibility given large cash reserves

stocks.recent_news: Iron-ore price hike effective April 5, 2026: Baila Lump +10.4% to Rs 5,300/t; Baila Fines +11.1% to Rs 4,500/t. Stock rose 4.64% on announcement to Rs 81.60. FY26 record production 53 MT (+21% YoY); sales 50.23 MT (+13% YoY). March 2026 production 5.35 MT (+50.7% YoY); sales 5.90 MT (+40.1% YoY). Stock at Rs 88.47 on April 21 — close to 52-week high Rs 88.07; market cap Rs 78,159 Cr. Declared interim dividend Rs 2.50 in Q3 (yield ~6.5%).
stocks.sector_context: Metals & mining constructive: rising spot prices for iron ore, supportive global commodity backdrop. Domestic steel demand robust — supports captive iron ore offtake. PSU mining theme in favor on dividend yield + production growth combination.
stocks.peer_comparison: Strong production growth (+21% YoY) vs Vedanta, Hindustan Zinc which are more diversified plays. Pricing power demonstrated by 10-11% hike absorption. Analysts predict 42.9% YoY PAT growth in Q4 FY26 — among best in PSU mining basket.
red_flags: Stock near 52-week high — limited near-term upside
Iron ore price hikes may face pushback from steel makers if global prices soften
Government PSU — periodic OFS overhang risk
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results expected — analyst consensus 42.9% YoY PAT growth
Final dividend announcement (history of generous payouts)
FY27 production guidance ramp toward 100 MT vision

stocks.recent_news: Board meeting scheduled April 27, 2026 for Q4 FY26 results and final dividend declaration (some sources indicate May 12 — date may be revised). Stock trading ~Rs 390 in April 2026 — down ~10% from 52-week high of Rs 540. Q3 FY26 declared dividend of Rs 5.50/share. Company absorbing rising explosives/diesel cost pressure; insulating downstream coal users from price escalation.
stocks.sector_context: Coal sector outlook mixed — softening seasonal demand post-winter, high inventories at thermal plants, intensifying competition from captive/commercial coal block winners eating into CIL share. Renewable energy transition narrative continues to weigh on long-term coal sentiment.
stocks.peer_comparison: Underperforming NMDC (which is near 52-week highs) on production growth and pricing power. Coal India faces structural challenges — competition from new commercial mining entrants — that NMDC's iron ore monopoly doesn't share.
red_flags: Analysts expect WEAK Q4 FY26 due to competition, high inventories, softer demand
Margin compression from rising explosive/diesel costs not passed through
Stock down ~10% from 52-week high — momentum negative
Long-term thermal coal transition concern remains overhang
Earnings event risk — results in 3 days (April 27)
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results + final dividend on April 27, 2026 — though analysts expect weak print
Final dividend (FY25 paid Rs 26.40/share total) — high yield support
Summer power demand uptick could lift offtake commentary
Any government policy/price pool revision in CIL's favor
